items.definition_en: An announcement is something said to tell people important news.
items.example_en: The teacher made an announcement about the test.

items.definition_en: An audience is a group of people who listen to or watch something.
items.example_en: The audience listened carefully to the speaker.

items.definition_en: A mic is a device used to make your voice louder.
items.example_en: She spoke into the mic during her speech.

items.definition_en: A speaker is a person who talks to a group of people.
items.example_en: The speaker started the presentation with a joke.

items.definition_en: A speech is a talk given to a group of people.
items.example_en: She gave a short speech at the ceremony.

items.definition_en: A question is something you ask to get information.
items.example_en: He asked a question after the talk.

items.definition_en: An answer is something you say when someone asks you a question.
items.example_en: She gave a clear answer to the question.

items.definition_en: A joke is something you say to make people laugh.
items.example_en: He started his talk with a funny joke.

items.definition_en: A pause is a short stop while speaking.
items.example_en: She made a pause to think before continuing.

items.definition_en: An opinion is what someone thinks or feels about something.
items.example_en: He shared his opinion during the discussion.

items.definition_en: A topic is the subject you talk about.
items.example_en: Today's topic is healthy eating.

items.definition_en: An introduction is the beginning part of a speech.
items.example_en: The introduction helped everyone understand the talk.

items.definition_en: A stage is a place where people stand and speak to an audience.
items.example_en: She walked onto the stage with confidence.

items.definition_en: A microphone is a tool that makes your voice louder.
items.example_en: He used the microphone during his speech.

items.definition_en: A presentation is when someone shows or explains something to a group.
items.example_en: Her presentation was very interesting.

items.definition_en: Applause is the sound people make by clapping their hands to show they enjoyed something.
items.example_en: The speaker received loud applause from the audience.

items.definition_en: A talk is a short speech or presentation to people.
items.example_en: He gave a talk about travel.

items.definition_en: Question time is when people can ask questions after a speech.
items.example_en: At the end, there was a question time for the audience.

items.definition_en: A panel is a small group of people talking in front of an audience.
items.example_en: The panel answered questions from students.

items.definition_en: A plan is a list of things you want to do or say.
items.example_en: She made a plan for her speech.
